_descriptor_type: A type of descriptor. One of 'simulation', 'trace', and 'perf'.
_architecture: A name of the available architectures (PARAMS.<architecture>) found in scarab repository, which defines the machine parameters of the default configuration.
_experiment: Attribution force-hit oracle vs perfect-D$ ceiling on SPEC06. Uses the branch_load_dep attribution framework to force an L1-hit-latency completion for exactly the recorded branch-delay (marked) loads, and compares the IPC / branch-stall recovery against forcing ALL loads to complete at L1 hit latency. Both arms share the identical completion path in dcache_stage.c dcache_cacheline_miss (op->done_cycle = cycle_count + DCACHE_CYCLES + extra_ld_latency; the memory request still issues so the line fills and MSHRs are respected); they differ ONLY in which loads qualify -- br_addr_replay_hit(line) for the marked set vs every load for perfect. So baseline (real cache) is the floor, all_hit is the oracle ceiling, and the marked_hit_* configs measure what fraction of that ceiling the attribution set alone recovers, at two coverage operating points. PREREQUISITE for the marked_hit_* configs: per-simpoint <bench>_<simpt>_braddr.csv from a prior SPEC06 attribute-record pass (branch_load_dep_attribute_record), present in bld_traces_kmhv3; a workload lacking one marks no loads and behaves as baseline.
_workload_manager: manual: run each exp scenario with all the simpoints at a time, slurm: Slurm schedules simulations based on its policy
_root_dir: Root directory mounted into the docker container home, for instance, the user's home directory
_application_dir: The path to the benchmark application directory used for tracing or simulation.
_scarab_path: The path to the scarab repository that contains the scarab binary used to run the experiment.
_traces_dir: The local directory containing all simpointed traces
_top_simpoint: Run only top 3 simpoints stored in 'workloads_top_simp.json' instead of the entire simpoints in 'workloads_db.json'
_simulations: SPEC CPU2006 integer + floating-point rate suites.
_configurations: Force-hit oracle comparison. baseline = real D-cache, no oracle (floor). marked_hit_cov90 / marked_hit_cov100 = attribution force-hit oracle (--branch_load_dep_addr_replay 1) restricted to the recorded branch-delay set covering 90%/100% of attributed stall cycles (--branch_load_dep_addr_replay_coverage). all_hit = perfect-D$ hit-latency oracle (--perfect_dcache_hit_latency 1), every load completes at L1 hit latency (ceiling). All oracles complete at DCACHE_CYCLES + extra_ld_latency and still issue the fill; the ONLY difference across the three is the set of loads that qualify. Coverage 1.0 = full recorded set; 0.9 = the canonical smallest-set operating point.

baseline._params: Real D-cache, no force-hit oracle. Floor of the comparison.

marked_hit_cov90._params: Attribution force-hit: marked (recorded branch-delay) loads complete at L1 hit latency, using the smallest line set covering 90% of attributed stall cycles.

marked_hit_cov100._params: Attribution force-hit: marked loads complete at L1 hit latency, using the full recorded branch-delay set (100% coverage). Upper bound of what the attribution set alone can recover.

all_hit._params: Perfect-D$ hit-latency oracle: EVERY load completes at L1 hit latency on a miss (still issues the fill). Ceiling of the comparison.

_collect_stats: Stats collection settings for ./sci --collect-stats. configs: optional list of configuration names to collect (null or omitted = all configurations).
_mem: Memory scheduling and collection settings for Slurm jobs.
